description: Add comprehensive mobile CSS media queries and enhance tap targets in frontend/src/index.css, plus make the Login page mobile-friendly
steps: In frontend/src/index.css, within the @media (max-width: 639px) phone breakpoint, add: .btn { min-height: 44px; } and .btn-sm { min-height: 38px; } to increase tap targets
In frontend/src/index.css, within the @media (max-width: 639px) phone breakpoint, add: .form-input { min-height: 44px; } for larger input tap targets
In frontend/src/index.css, add smooth scroll behavior for mobile: @media (max-width: 1023px) { html { scroll-behavior: smooth; } * { -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; } }

In frontend/src/index.css, add a new responsive helper class .responsive-grid that uses CSS to override inline grid styles on mobile. Add: @media (max-width: 639px) { [style*='gridTemplateColumns'] { grid-template-columns: 1fr !important; } } - This uses attribute selector to force all inline gridTemplateColumns to single column on phone
In frontend/src/index.css, within the phone breakpoint add: .card { margin-bottom: 8px; } to give cards breathing room when stacked

In frontend/src/index.css, add at the tablet breakpoint @media (max-width: 1023px): [style*='gridTemplateColumns'][style*='repeat(4'] { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr) !important; } and [style*='gridTemplateColumns'][style*='repeat(5'] { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr) !important; } - this makes 4 and 5 column grids become 2 columns on tablet

In frontend/src/pages/Login.tsx, modify the outer flex container to become single-column on mobile by changing the outer div style to add flexDirection responsive behavior. Wrap the left branding panel in a className='login-left' div and the right form panel in a className='login-right' div. Add CSS rules in index.css: @media (max-width: 768px) { .login-left { display: none !important; } .login-right { flex: 1 !important; padding: 24px 20px !important; } } - This hides the branding panel and makes the form full-width on mobile

In frontend/src/pages/Login.tsx, add a compact mobile header that shows only on mobile (shows logo + CareVista name above the form) when .login-left is hidden. Add className='login-mobile-header' and CSS: .login-mobile-header { display: none; } @media (max-width: 768px) { .login-mobile-header { display: flex; ... } }

In frontend/src/pages/Login.tsx, change the demo accounts grid from gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(2, 1fr)' to use a className with responsive override so it becomes single-column on small phones: add className='login-demo-grid' and CSS @media (max-width: 400px) { .login-demo-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr !important; } }
acceptance_criteria: Running `cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no errors
Buttons have min-height 44px on screens under 640px via CSS
Form inputs have min-height 44px on screens under 640px via CSS
All inline gridTemplateColumns styles are forced to single column on phones via CSS attribute selector override
Login page hides left branding panel on screens under 768px and shows full-width form
Login page shows a compact mobile header with logo when on mobile
findings: Build passes cleanly. All CSS additions and Login.tsx changes verified via npm run build. No TypeScript errors.
